---
title: "nonverbal communication check-in"
format: html
---

## Nonverbal Communication Check-In

Body language affects both how you feel and how others respond to you. Eye contact, posture, facial expression, and tone can communicate confidence, stress, anger, or withdrawal - even when words say something else.

In recovery, nonverbal awareness is useful in conflict, support conversations, and high-risk social settings. Small shifts in posture and breathing can reduce escalation and improve connection.

Interpersonal stress is a common relapse risk. Communication habits, emotional reactivity, and body language all affect how quickly conflict escalates and how supported you feel afterward.

When communication becomes clearer and more regulated, stress drops and problem-solving improves. These skills protect recovery by reducing isolation, resentment, and impulsive reactions during difficult interactions.

::: callout
### Reflection Questions
1. What does my body language look like when I feel triggered?
2. What nonverbal changes help me feel steadier?
3. What signal do I want to communicate more often (calm, openness, confidence)?
:::

| Situation | My usual nonverbal pattern | Alternative I will practice |
|---|---|---|
| | | |
| | | |
| | | |
